title: A wireless network of adaptable tap sensitive surfaces
abstract: A Wireless Network of Adaptable Tap Sensitive Surfaces A network of hard surfaces (11) adapted to detect a tap and enabled to interact with each other or with devices (14) connected to the network in a wireless fashion. The detection of a tap on vertical and horizontal surfaces like boards and tables is achieved using piezoelectric sensors and signal conditioners coupled with a tap detection module. The location of the tap is determined based on time differences of arrival of the tap signals to the sensors. The connected devices (12, 14) are adapted to execute actions based on the location of the tap. A wireless adapter interfaced with a single board computer (12) allows a surface to be networked with other similar surfaces (11) or devices capable of wireless communication. Standard mesh networking protocols are used for larger area networks using only a wireless adapter. A wireless local area network switch (13) can be used for smaller networks.
claims: Claims 1. A network of tap sensitive surfaces (10) comprising -a plurality of arbitrary surfaces (11) rendered to detect taps on the surface; -a control device (12) comprising further of a means to detect taps, a means to localize a detected tap on the surface (11) it comes from, and a means to communicate wlrelessly; -a programmable device (14) comprising further of a means to generate a response on the device (14) itself, on devices interfaced via the internet (15) or an a device interfaced with a surface (11) according to the detected taps and their locations on the surface. 2. The network in Claim 1 wherein an arbitrary surface (11) is rendered to detect taps by attaching a plurality of piezoelectric sensors (30) to its surface (11) and comprising further of a signal conditioner (31) and a tap detector module (32). 3. The network in Claim 2 wherein the signal conditioner (31) comprises further of an amplifier. 4. The network in Claims 2 or 3 wherein the tap detector module (32) comprises of at least one comparator circuit comprising further of a diode, resistor, capacitor, comparator and a logic gate. 5. The network in any of the previous Claims wherein the sensors (30) are attached to the rear surface (11B) of the surface designated for tapping (11A). 6. The network in Claim 5 wherein the sensors (30) are attached to a recessed area on the rear surface (11B). 7. The network in any of the previous Claims wherein the inactive surface (11B) is also designated as the tapping surface in place of the front surface (11A). 8. The network in any of the previous Claims wherein the inactive surface (118) is also designated as the tapping surface in combination with the front surface (11 A). 9. The network in any of the previous Claims wherein the means to localize a tap comprises of a single-board computer (34) programmed to implement a time difference of arrival technique for the signals coming from all the sensors (30) on a particular surface (11) and detected by the tap module (32). 10. The network in any of the previous Claims wherein the means to communicate wiretessly comprises of a wireless adapter (35) and a wireless local area network switch (13). 11. The network in Claims 1 to 10 wherein the means to communicate wirelessly comprises of a wireless adapter (35) combined with a mesh networking protocol. 12. The network in any of the previous Claims wherein at least one tap sensitive surface (11) is configured for restaurant operations with an appropriate graphical interface (50) for the possible actions that can be generated by tapping on a designated area on the surface (11). 13. The network in any of the previous Claims wherein at least one tap sensftive surface (11) is configured for a classroom with an appropriate graphical interface (50) for the possible actions that can be generated by tapping on a designated area on the surface (11). 14. The network in any of the previous Claims wherein at least one tap sensitive surface (11) is configured for operating a projector (70). 15. The network in any of the previous Claims wherein at least one surface (11) is configured as an advertising billboard with an appropriate graphical interface (50) for the possible actions that can be generated by tapping on a designated area on the surface (11). 16. The network in any of the previous Claims wherein at least one tap sensitive surface (11) is configured for operating a digital device connected directly or wirelessly to the network (10). 17. A process to organize an arbitrary set of surfaces (11) into a network (10) of interacting surfaces comprising -rendering each arbitrary surface (11) to be tap sensitive using piezoelectric sensors (30); -detecting a tap on any of the surfaces (11) with any one of the sensors (30) on it; -determining the location of the tap on a particular surface from the difference in the time of arrival of the tap signal to the sensors (30) attached to it; -determining which surface generated the tap; -determining the order in which the taps occurred; -assigning an action to the tap depending on its location on the surface (11) and order of occurrence; -communicating the desired action to another surface (11) or programmable device (14) on the network (10) via a wireless adapter (35). 18. The process in Claim 17 wherein the process of detecting a tap comprises further of a calibration process involving a plurality of taps on a plurality of selected locations on the surface (11) and averaging the signals from each location to set a threshold value for the tap detection module (32). 19. The process in Claims 17 and 18 wherein a computer program for any of the steps is implemented on the single board computer (34) in the control device (12), on the programmable device (14) or any combination thereof.
